pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run against Java 17 semantics, failing to utilize or enforce Java 21 features and compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add lombok-mapstruct-binding dependency to resolve annotation processor conflicts between Lombok and MapStruct.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this binding, MapStruct and Lombok may conflict during compilation, leading to missing getters/setters or compilation errors when MapStruct tries to access Lombok-generated methods.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add lombok-mapstruct-binding to the annotation processor paths in the maven-compiler-plugin.
pom.xml.why_exists: The binding must be on the annotation processor path to function correctly during compilation; omitting it results in the same Lombok/MapStruct conflicts as missing the dependency.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target compiler options with the unified release option.
pom.xml.why_exists: While source/target still work, using 'release' is the modern standard for cross-compilation and ensures consistent module path behavior in newer JDKs; however, this is often considered polishment unless strict compliance is enforced. Given the context of JDK 21 migration, this is a best-practice update.
